subject: english
title: Tiếng Anh THCS Ngoại Ngữ (UMS) — Đề trải nghiệm
intro: Đề trải nghiệm vào lớp 6 THCS Ngoại Ngữ (UMS) — 30 câu trắc nghiệm: ngữ pháp/từ vựng, hoàn thành đoạn (cloze), đọc hiểu, hoàn thành hội thoại, câu đồng nghĩa và ghép biển báo. Nguồn: Trung tâm ngoại ngữ Bigtree Land.
source: Đề trải nghiệm vào 6 THCS Ngoại Ngữ — Bigtree Land (không ghi năm)

passages.title: Film review: The Impossible
passages.body:
I've just seen The Impossible, directed by J.A. Bayona. The film, which mixes elements of drama, thriller and disaster, is the incredible story of an ordinary family. The Bennetts travel to Thailand to (9) ______ Christmas. All too soon their idyllic holiday is brought to an abrupt end when a tsunami hits the resort where they (10) ______.
The images of the giant wave washing over the resort are rather realistic and make you feel as if you were drowned, too. Stars Naomi Watts and Ewan McGregor are fantastic as the brave parents, struggling (11) ______ to save their children. Tom Holland, (12) ______ plays the eldest boy, gives an extremely moving performance. What I like most about this film is its human quality. The Bennett family's epic struggle for survival is a celebration of the power of love, sending a message of hope to other people caught in difficult situations. At the end of the film, I felt rather exhausted (13) ______ satisfied at the same time. Although some of the images of devastation and suffering are very hard to watch, I'd recommend everyone (14) ______ and see this film.

passages.title: The Year of the Cat
passages.body:
Many East Asian nations are about to have their Lunar New Year's parties. In China, Japan, and others, they will welcome the Year of the Rabbit. But in Vietnam, it will be the Year of the Cat. There is no clear explanation for why the Vietnamese chose the cat instead of the rabbit, but there are some good ideas.
Ngo Huong Giang, an East Asian language expert, said one reason might be that the word in Mandarin for "Year of the Rabbit" sounds similar to the Vietnamese word for cat.
Nguyen Hieu Tin, who studies Vietnamese culture, said the answer may come from the rice fields. "Rice is a huge part of life in Vietnam. But there are many rats that will eat the rice. Luckily, we have cats which can hunt them," he says.
Poet Le Thanh Hue provides a simpler reason, "To the Vietnamese, rabbits and rats look like each other. And we don't want two similar animals in the zodiac." He also notes that keeping rabbits is not common in Vietnam.
Hoang Thi Huong Giang, an office worker in Hanoi, said she had never paid attention to why Vietnam celebrates a different animal than the rest of the world. But she believes that life is a breeze for those who were born in the Year of the Cat, like her. They tend to enjoy smoother paths to success and generally live more comfortable lives compared to others. "It seems to be true that those who are born in the Year of the Cat are often more active, hard-working and friendlier." Giang added proudly.

passages.title: A foreign exchange student
passages.body:
Claire: Tomorrow I'm going to the airport with my family to pick up the foreign exchange student we're hosting.
Alan: (20) ______ I mean, you love meeting new people and doing things that are out of the ordinary. Having a student from Vietnam live with you for a semester will bring both.
Claire: Of course. Can you imagine how different her life must be from ours?
Alan: Personally, I'm a little let down that you're not hosting a boy. (21) ______
Claire: You'll still have that chance. She looks very sporty. (22) ______
Alan: That's music to my ears. Perhaps I'll enjoy hosting a foreign exchange student as much as you will.

Le Thanh Hue

options.text: excellent hunters
lucky creatures
helpful to people

questions.stem: What does Le Thanh Hue say about rabbits and rats?

options.text: Vietnamese feel the same about them.
Their appearance is close to the same.
They may have quite similar habits.

questions.stem: What does Hoang Thi Huong Giang mean by saying "life is a breeze" for people born in the Year of the Cat?

options.text: Other people are not as talented as they are.
They face fewer difficulties than other people.
They have more possessions than other people.

questions.stem: What is the passage mainly about?

options.text: Why Vietnam has the Year of the Cat
People's thoughts on the Year of the Cat
The importance of the Year of the Cat

questions.stem:
Choose the sentence that is closest in meaning to the original sentence:
"Sally spent a week preparing costumes for the Halloween party."

options.text: It took Sally a week to get her clothes ready for the Halloween party.
Sally had to finish making clothes for the Halloween party in a week.
A week was enough for Sally to design clothes for the Halloween party.

questions.stem:
Choose the sentence that is closest in meaning to the original sentence:
"People nowadays have less time for family than they used to."

options.text: The amount of time spent with family has decreased these days.
People have paid less attention to family time recently.
Nowadays people don't have enough time for their family.

questions.stem:

"Children should establish a routine of using dustbins to collect rubbish."

options.text: Children should familiarize themselves with putting trash into dustbins.
Equipping children with taking out trash as a daily routine is advisable.
Children's sense of duty to throw trash into dustbins is a matter of routine.

questions.stem:

"This project has been put on ice until the school has enough resources."

options.text: Doing this project is like skating on thin ice due to the school's lack of resources.
Until the school has all the necessary resources, this project is being put on hold.
As soon as the school has the needed resources, this project will see the light of day.
